user.title: Yorkshire Water
user.description: Provide your Yorkshire Water email and password, plus the URLs of the two companion stealth browser add-ons. The integration uses one of them each refresh; the Yorkshire Water session has a hard 30-minute server cap so a fresh login is the only way to stay autonomous. Choose which engine drives the login via Options after setup.

data_description.playwright_url: URL of the Patchright Stealth Browser add-on. Default points at the local add-on on port 3001.
data_description.nodriver_url: URL of the nodriver Stealth Browser add-on. Default points at the local add-on on port 3002.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Yorkshire Water
reauth_confirm.description: The stored credentials no longer work. Provide them again to continue.

error.playwright_unreachable: Could not reach the Playwright server. Make sure the Playwright Stealth Browser add-on is installed, started, and reachable at the URL above.
error.unknown: Something unexpected went wrong. Check the Home Assistant log for details.

abort.reauth_no_entry: The original config entry could not be found.
abort.reauth_account_mismatch: Reauth must use the same email as the original entry. Remove and re-add the integration if you want to change account.

data_description.browser_engine: Which stealth browser add-on drives the login each refresh. Patchright (Playwright fork with a patched Chromium) tends to win against Akamai; nodriver (Python; raw CDP) tends to win against Cloudflare. Switch freely; the storage profile is shared.
data_description.playwright_url: URL of the Patchright Stealth Browser add-on. Used when Browser engine is set to playwright.
data_description.nodriver_url: URL of the nodriver Stealth Browser add-on. Used when Browser engine is set to nodriver.
data_description.refresh_time: Local time of the first refresh each day. With more than one refresh per day, the day is divided evenly from this time forward.
data_description.refreshes_per_day: How many refreshes per day. Yorkshire Water's upstream cadence is not publicly documented; once per day at a chosen time keeps the integration well below the reCAPTCHA-score threshold and is the recommended default.
